skill.name: nanobanana-ppt-skills
skill.description: Guidance for creating AI-assisted PowerPoint presentations from source documents, including slide structure and image styling.
skill.summary: Create structured PowerPoint presentation plans from documents with styled image guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged two hardcoded URLs and two weak cryptography patterns. Review found the URLs are documentation and source repository references, and the cryptography alerts are keyword false positives in descriptive text. No executable code, prompt injection attempt, data exfiltration path, or cryptographic implementation was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Findings Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The URLs at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22 point to the source repository and more information. They are static Markdown references, not executable network requests or data transmission logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only Markdown and front matter. The URLs are repository references with no code that can perform network access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md lines 3 and 20, but those lines contain descriptive presentation text. No weak cryptographic algorithm, crypto API, hash function, or encryption workflow is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are plain text metadata and instructions. There is no cryptographic operation to evaluate.

content.value_statement: Teams often need to turn source documents into clear presentation plans quickly. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through document analysis, slide structure, and styled image planning.
actual_capabilities: Guides AI-assisted planning for PowerPoint presentations.
Supports document analysis before building slide outlines.
Helps define slide themes, visual direction, and image styles.
Provides a simple workflow for presentation generation prompts.
Can be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
limitations: The skill file does not include executable presentation generation code.
It does not provide PowerPoint templates, sample decks, or image assets.
Generated slides still depend on the host AI tool and available document context.
Users must verify facts, branding, and visual accessibility before delivery.

use_cases.title: Convert Reports Into Slide Plans
use_cases.description: Turn a business report into a concise slide outline with key messages, section flow, and visual notes.

use_cases.title: Prepare Branded Client Presentations
use_cases.description: Create a presentation plan that includes tone, slide structure, and image styling for a client review.

use_cases.title: Draft Teaching Decks From Notes
use_cases.description: Transform lecture notes into a clear classroom deck plan with supporting visuals and topic progression.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Deck Outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple presentation structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nanobanana-ppt-skills to turn this document into a PowerPoint outline. Include slide titles, one main message per slide, and suggested visuals.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Document for Slides
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that needs content selection.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nanobanana-ppt-skills to analyze this source document. Identify the strongest presentation narrative, remove low-value details, and propose a 10-slide deck.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Visual Style and Images
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on design direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nanobanana-ppt-skills to create a slide plan with image styling guidance. Define a consistent visual theme, image prompts, and layout notes for each slide.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Executive Presentation Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for audience-specific presentation planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nanobanana-ppt-skills to convert this material into an executive presentation brief. Include the audience goal, slide sequence, key evidence, speaker notes, and image direction.

output_examples.input: A product launch brief with goals, audience, timeline, and feature notes.
output: A 12-slide launch deck outline with an opening narrative, market context, product value, timeline, and closing ask.
Each slide includes a main message, supporting content, and a suggested visual style.

output_examples.input: A quarterly business review document with metrics and commentary.
output: A concise executive deck plan that prioritizes performance trends, risks, decisions, and next steps.
The plan includes charts to create, talking points, and places where source evidence should be verified.

output_examples.input: Training notes for a workshop on a new internal process.
output: A teaching deck structure with learning objectives, concept slides, examples, practice prompts, and recap slides.
Visual guidance explains where diagrams, screenshots, and simple process graphics would help.

best_practices: Provide the source document, target audience, presentation goal, and desired slide count.
Ask for one main message per slide before requesting detailed visuals.
Review generated claims, charts, and brand details against approved source material.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a final deck without providing source content or audience context.
Do not rely on generated text for legal, financial, or compliance statements without review.
Do not request dense slides that copy long document sections without synthesis.

faq.question: Does this skill create a PowerPoint file directly?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance for AI-assisted presentation planning. File creation depends on the host tool and available integrations.

faq.question: Can it analyze long documents?
faq.answer: Yes, when the host AI tool can access the document content. Very long documents may need summaries or selected sections.

faq.question: Does it include visual assets?
faq.answer: No. It can guide image style and visual direction, but the skill file does not include image assets.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains only Markdown guidance and repository links. No executable code or prompt injection was found.

faq.question: What should users verify after generation?
faq.answer: Users should verify factual accuracy, source attribution, brand compliance, accessibility, and any sensitive business claims.
